beats.prompt: Two retrievals of the ρ definition from the last lesson. Match each statement to its value.

pairs.left: ρ always lies in the interval…

feedback.correct: ρ ∈ [−1,1], and ρ = Cov/√(Var_X·Var_Y) — the bound the geometry is about to tighten.
hints: Both are the L4 ρ definition: the interval it lives in, and the denominator that normalizes it.
ρ normalizes Cov by the product of the SDs, which is √(Var_X·Var_Y).
ρ ∈ [−1,1]; ρ = Cov / √(Var_X·Var_Y).

beats.prompt: x is 0.8-correlated with both y and z (ρ(x,y) = ρ(x,z) = 4/5). Can ρ(y,z) be anything in [−1,1]?

options: Yes — y and z are free, any value in [−1,1]

Yes — y and z are free, any value in [−1.note: Let's test it — correlations aren't free of each other. If x is strongly tied to both y and z, they can't be too unalike; the correlation matrix must be PSD, which fences ρ(y,z) (#10).

Up to 1, but down to −1.note: Let's test it — the top is right (it can reach 1), but the floor isn't −1: the PSD condition lifts the minimum up to 7/25 (#10).

No — pinned between 7/25 and 1.note: Good instinct — the PSD-determinant condition bounds it: ρ(y,z) ∈ [7/25, 1] (#10).

beats.prompt: Quick reminder before the geometry — read the card, then continue.

interaction.title: A valid correlation matrix must be PSD
interaction.body: A correlation matrix is positive semidefinite (PSD): for the 3×3 case [[1,ρ₁₂,ρ₁₃],[ρ₁₂,1,ρ₂₃],[ρ₁₃,ρ₂₃,1]], PSD requires its determinant ≥ 0. Solving det = 1 + 2ρ₁₂ρ₁₃ρ₂₃ − ρ₁₂² − ρ₁₃² − ρ₂₃² ≥ 0 for the third correlation pins it to a range. The determinant equals exactly 0 at the two attainable bounds.

feedback.correct: Valid correlation matrix ⇒ PSD ⇒ det ≥ 0; solving det ≥ 0 for the third ρ gives its range.
hints: Read the card.
PSD ⇒ determinant ≥ 0; the bounds are where it equals 0.
Continue when ready.

beats.prompt: Two vectors are fixed at ρ = 4/5 to x. Sweep the third vector and TRY to make y, z anti-correlated (ρ = −1). Watch the dot hit a wall.

hero.structuralReadout: ρ(y,z) can't drop below 7/25 — the PSD floor. Range [7/25, 1].

feedback.correct: You found the floor — ρ(y,z) can't drop below 7/25. With ρ₁ = ρ₂ = 4/5 the attainable range is [7/25, 1], and the determinant hits 0 at each end.
hints: Drag the third vector toward 'anti-correlated' and watch the dot on the number-line.
The dot slides toward the floor and STOPS — it can't reach −1 because the matrix must stay PSD.

beats.prompt: Warm up with the ρ definition (spaced recall from L4). With Cov = 12, Var(X) = 9, Var(Y) = 25, what is ρ(X,Y)?

feedback.correct: ρ = 12/√(9·25) = 12/15 = 4/5 — the L4 definition, fresh from this lesson's opener.
hints: ρ = Cov/√(Var·Var), which you just retrieved.

beats.prompt: One more reminder before the mastery — read the card, then continue.

interaction.title: The optimal hedge is Cov/Var — no ρ, no √
interaction.body: To minimize the variance of A − h·B, the optimal hedge ratio is h* = Cov(A,B)/Var(B). It is a single division — a covariance over a variance — and it never needs the correlation or a square root. (Equivalently h* = ρ·σ_A/σ_B, but Cov/Var avoids ρ entirely.)

feedback.correct: h* = Cov(A,B)/Var(B): one division, no ρ, no √.
hints: Read the card.
Hedge = Cov over Var, not Cov over √(Var·Var).
Continue when ready.

beats.prompt: Transfer challenge: now x is 3/5-correlated with both y and z (ρ(x,y)=ρ(x,z)=3/5). Part A: give the min and max possible ρ(y,z). Part B: for n=4 variables with ALL pairwise correlations equal to ρ, give the minimum ρ. Interleave: long 1 share A, short h shares B, with Cov(A,B)=−8 and Var(B)=4 — give the variance-minimizing hedge h*.

interaction.scenario: Part A: ρ(x,y)=ρ(x,z)=3/5 (a Pythagorean pair: √(1−9/25)=4/5 stays rational), so the PSD determinant pins ρ(y,z) to a range. Part B: four variables with every pairwise correlation equal to ρ — the equicorrelation floor is −1/(n−1). Interleave: the minimum-variance hedge is h*=Cov/Var, a single division, no ρ and no √.

feedback.correct: Part A: centre ρ₁ρ₂=9/25, spread √(1−9/25)·√(1−9/25)=(4/5)(4/5)=16/25, so min=9/25−16/25=−7/25 and max=9/25+16/25=1. Part B: det=(1−ρ)²(1+3ρ)≥0 ⇒ 1+3ρ≥0 ⇒ ρ≥−1/3 (general −1/(n−1) with n=4). Interleave: h*=Cov/Var=−8/4=−2 — one division, no ρ.
hints: Part A: the third ρ isn't free; min/max are centre±spread from the PSD determinant. Part B: n mutually-equal correlations bottom out at −1/(n−1). Interleave: the hedge is Cov/Var, no ρ or √.
Part A: centre=ρ₁ρ₂=9/25, spread=√(1−9/25)²=(4/5)(4/5)=16/25. Part B: with n=4 the floor is −1/(4−1). Interleave: (−8)/4 — don't normalize into a correlation.

beats.prompt: Mastery — three parts. Part A: the attainable range of ρ(y,z). Part B: the equicorrelation floor. Interleave: the optimal hedge.

interaction.scenario: Part A: ρ(x,y) = ρ(x,z) = 4/5. Give the minimum and maximum possible ρ(y,z) (the Pythagorean pair 4/5, 3/5 keeps every √ rational). Part B: three variables with ALL pairwise correlations equal to ρ — give the minimum possible ρ. Interleave: long 1 share A, short h shares B, with Cov(A,B) = −6 and Var(B) = 9 — give the variance-minimizing hedge h*.

feedback.correct: Part A: with ρ₁ = ρ₂ = 4/5 the leftover root is √(9/25) = 3/5, so min = 16/25 − 9/25 = 7/25 and max = 16/25 + 9/25 = 1. Part B: det = (1−ρ)²(1+2ρ) ≥ 0 ⇒ 1+2ρ ≥ 0 ⇒ ρ ≥ −1/2 (general −1/(n−1)). Interleave: h* = Cov/Var = −6/9 = −2/3 — a single division, no ρ.
hints: Part A: the third ρ isn't free; min/max come from the PSD determinant. Part B: three mutually-negative correlations fight each other. Interleave: the hedge is Cov/Var, no ρ or √.
Part A: min = 16/25 − (3/5)(3/5), max = 16/25 + (3/5)(3/5). Part B: det (1−ρ)²(1+2ρ) ≥ 0 ⇒ 1+2ρ ≥ 0. Interleave: (−6)/9 — don't normalize into a correlation.
Part A: min = 7/25, max = 1 (#10). Part B: ρ ≥ −1/2 (#11); general −1/(n−1) (#12). Interleave: h* = −2/3 (#13).

beats.interviewNote: Interview framing: the 3-variable correlation range is a Green-Book / Jane-Street classic — the PSD-determinant det = 1+2ρ₁ρ₂ρ−ρ₁²−ρ₂²−ρ² ≥ 0 fences the third correlation, and the equicorrelation floor is −1/(n−1) (so n correlated assets can't all be strongly negatively correlated). The hedge h* = Cov(A,B)/Var(B) is the minimum-variance hedge ratio — Cov over Var, never the correlation alone.

feedback.correct: A valid correlation matrix must be PSD, so the third correlation is fenced (here [7/25, 1]), and equal pairwise correlations can't drop below −1/(n−1). Next: the synthesis capstone.
hints: Read each recap card.
PSD ⇒ det ≥ 0 ⇒ the third ρ is bounded; the equicorrelation floor is −1/(n−1).
Tap to continue.
